            301.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You are useful - for now. And we may yet find a way to gut the prince before he can fly free.!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPath

              186.Origin_Intro_Laezel: But I cannot forever abide your disrespect. Cross me too often, and you will face the coming madness without me. [End]

              Metadata

      266.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Besides. Why kill him, when we can torture him with the death of his beloved prince?

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: b5e532e5-3949-e927-7bd6-600f89f5c061Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h154013f4gde73g4075gb758gaf7f08f78041Text UUID: 6247fa57-4ada-4d3b-bf81-9421b4a7fcebGroup ID: 6dca9e41-187e-b0e4-c8ee-2aa3a6eb46f0Group Index: 1
      • +

      368.Origin_Intro_Laezel: He will be known as an eternal Warrior of Legend. Not just by the githyanki, but by the psalmists and scholars of every plane.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: fc76fd6a-c8c5-e695-234a-e8b9982b4679Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: heedbb7e3g5f14g4e9bg9766g90b647870926Text UUID: ba33feb1-35a1-4faa-8449-a1e2119eb6cdGroup ID: edb369a8-f15d-8533-3bcc-13adfe5d489cGroup Index: 1
      • +
        247.Origin_Intro_Laezel: For a thousand years, he waited. For a thousand years, he remained faithful to Gith and her royal spawn.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: a559fe00-156c-895b-9de9-7106deb337e1Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h33ec8623g12f4g4a34gb829gc29ab4a43d45Text UUID: 9c85e0b3-7704-4694-9586-958bf64eddceGroup ID: edb369a8-f15d-8533-3bcc-13adfe5d489cGroup Index: 2
        • +
          314.Origin_Intro_Laezel: We have done him proud - as you have done me.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: d45ba147-a759-85b0-8a21-7745e53b3250Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h80ac7e41ge4e8g4782gac31gdcafaebffed4Text UUID: 6ca7ec08-36a4-4854-b5d8-8ee25c465986Group ID: edb369a8-f15d-8533-3bcc-13adfe5d489cGroup Index: 3
          • +
            28.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You know what to do. When we next enter the Astral Prism, we will break Orpheus free.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0bc9284b-493e-6351-1575-96d15f7aadfcSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: hd350015dgf85bg46ecg9cb0g13b53c56798eText UUID: f1842a28-d144-467f-b87b-ed593920186aGroup ID: edb369a8-f15d-8533-3bcc-13adfe5d489cGroup Index: 4

      42.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I've half a mind to tear your limbs off, one by one. By Mother Gith's silver sword, what were you thinking?

      Metadata

          139.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Is my life nothing but a game of lanceboard to you? Is it your habit to smash the pieces as your finishing move?

      127.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Orpheus' greatest champion, snuffed out before he could see the Prince of the Comet tear through Realmspace and beyond.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 54876cc3-a2c0-fca1-22ff-4e9860358ebbSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h71e03d5dg7562g4b51g92ebg3d0e2dc61c8bText UUID: b32bc182-0745-4c7c-af50-1d545aba0433Timeline Phase Index: 141Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 54876cc3-a2c0-fca1-22ff-4e9860358ebbTimeline Phase Duration: 16.83sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [878.17s-895s] | @ 882.17s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [878.17s-895s] | @ 883.07s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [878.17s-895s] | @ 885.3s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 4: Shot 1 [878.17s-895s] | @ 887.29s | speaker 0 | Happy (2)Timeline Emotion 5: Shot 1 [878.17s-895s] | @ 888.89s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)Timeline Emotion 6: Shot 1 [878.17s-895s] | @ 893.46s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 7: Shot 1 [895s-899.1s] | @ 895s | speaker 0 | Angry (8)Group ID: 54876cc3-a2c0-fca1-22ff-4e9860358ebbGroup Index: 0

            156.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Githyanki knights. The riders that chased the nautiloid. They are the commissars and enforcers of my Queen Vlaakith's will.

            Metadata

                      180.Origin_Intro_Laezel: The ghaik are our mortal enemy. It is not unusual for the kith'rak to give chase.Githyanki

                        132.Origin_Intro_Laezel: To penetrate the Hells? This is unusual.

                        Metadata

                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 58349c61-cb5e-1810-6ea1-6d36d947c383Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h8fce9977gdad1g4e89g8311g02e1e9b85a10Text UUID: 5b75a6b2-b638-4bb2-9a8d-10d5e1054d44Group ID: 7b3f57ae-3a5a-8c4b-89bf-49261611fccaGroup Index: 1
                        • •
                          146.Origin_Intro_Laezel: But let us not question the wisdom of our Queen. We can see but to the horizon. Vlaakith's sight pierces the many planes. [End]

                          Metadata

                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 6059e2f2-603c-f5f3-15c5-b1b5859d2c25Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h9439ea25g609bg4f00g9aadg6f7176b1af89Text UUID: 0be1155b-d312-4182-b7e6-26f0fc454351End Node: TrueGroup ID: 7b3f57ae-3a5a-8c4b-89bf-49261611fccaGroup Index: 2
                    • +
                      136.Origin_Intro_Laezel: The ghaik are my kind's mortal enemy. It is not unusual for the kith'rak to give chase.

                      Metadata

                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 5b001dd6-5b39-2111-7dfa-477e109616c8Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: hc382f181ga90ag4451gbd07g9de043b9032aText UUID: 762eec6b-26fa-4d61-bdb5-71df35452b11Timeline Phase Index: 191Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 5b001dd6-5b39-2111-7dfa-477e109616c8Timeline Phase Duration: 18.57sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1303.52s-1322.09s] | @ 1309.45s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [1303.52s-1322.09s] | @ 1312.08s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [1303.52s-1322.09s] | @ 1313.54s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 4: Shot 1 [1303.52s-1322.09s] | @ 1314.41s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 5: Shot 1 [1303.52s-1322.09s] | @ 1315.68s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 6: Shot 1 [1303.52s-1322.09s] | @ 1317.41s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 7: Shot 1 [1303.52s-1322.09s] | @ 1320.38s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)Timeline Emotion 8: Shot 1 [1322.09s-1331.13s] | @ 1322.09s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)Group ID: 5b001dd6-5b39-2111-7dfa-477e109616c8Group Index: 0
                      • +
                        295.Origin_Intro_Laezel: To penetrate the Hells? This is unusual.

                        Metadata

                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: c41462fd-38dc-0868-5f01-878ee85c37c5Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h60b3b7e6gf2d6g4e34g9435gd2301504104fText UUID: 597290f2-ad39-4d75-943e-b1180723a1e6Group ID: 5b001dd6-5b39-2111-7dfa-477e109616c8Group Index: 1
                        • •
                          66.Origin_Intro_Laezel: But I am not one to question the wisdom of my Queen. I can see but to the horizon. Vlaakith's sight pierces the many planes. [End]

                    87.Player: What are those dragons to the githyanki? Allies? Mounts? Pets?Dragonborn

                    Flags and Tags

                    checktype Tag | speaker 1 Player
                    • Dragonborn {39783f17-8484-46a6-aa3b-f3d51122e5f3} = True

                    Metadata

                    Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 32ee6e7c-3d34-21e8-e805-d90af535ece6Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hac25ca34g8d14g4c46gb0e9g7f6d0169275eText UUID: 014bc4b9-39f8-4921-9e15-007b4fe7d766Show Once: True
                    • +
                      329.Origin_Intro_Laezel: They are gifts to my Queen from the goddess Tiamat herself. They reside in the great city of Tu'narath, awaiting the privilege of battle.

                      Metadata

                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: def1da47-591f-96b8-dc2e-038ec8453d1aSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h011753d9ge86fg41f1g942cg43995abfa69fText UUID: 929890a7-d4bc-4484-8cb1-2882f7f253f8Timeline Phase Index: 118Timeline Dialog Node UUID: def1da47-591f-96b8-dc2e-038ec8453d1aTimeline Phase Duration: 12sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [689.24s-698.79s] | @ 690.3s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [689.24s-698.79s] | @ 691.84s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [689.24s-698.79s] | @ 696s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)Timeline Emotion 4: Shot 1 [701.24s-708.21s] | @ 701.24s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)
                      • +
                        286.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Do not pity them. They reap great riches from their service - gold and otherwise.!CAMP_GoblinHunt_State_LaezelPartner

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 1 Player
                        • CAMP_GoblinHunt_State_LaezelPartner {b825d5c1-dd57-b04d-2be4-c323d8e2629d} = False

                          219.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Dragons' blood flows in you, does it not? Perhaps you might be availed of the... riches I offer.

              311.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Vlaakith bestows no greater honour. To wield a kith'rak's silver sword is my destiny.

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: d178a57d-cf07-7cca-6800-a1159fa01cacSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h907a0c8ag524bg4aebgbfd9g00676a4e81e5Text UUID: 7fdc9086-f996-4064-850a-dcfe96fba337Timeline Phase Index: 113Timeline Dialog Node UUID: d178a57d-cf07-7cca-6800-a1159fa01cacTimeline Phase Duration: 7.2sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [653.49s-659.96s] | @ 656.32s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [653.49s-659.96s] | @ 657.59s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [653.49s-659.96s] | @ 658.62s | speaker 0 | Disgust (128)Timeline Emotion 4: Shot 1 [660.69s-667.46s] | @ 660.69s | speaker 0 | Sad (32)
              • See Node 331 (Origin_Intro_Laezel) I will earn my Queen's favour. And I will conquer every layer of Hell, should she command it.

            110.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Steel and iron may be no match for him. But a githyanki knight's silver sword would pierce him through - even amid the fires of Avernus.

                    104.Origin_Intro_Laezel: They are githyanki knights, the commissars and enforcers of my Queen Vlaakith's will. To die in her name is their last act of reverence.

                  83.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I've a mind to end you here and now, but you either believe your own words - or have suddenly mastered the duplicitous arts.

                  Metadata

              118.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I have not failed you yet and I have no intentions of it now. I don't need you to believe me for it to be true.

            260.Origin_Intro_Laezel: The Tears follow behind it. Rocky bodies tumbling through the Sea of Night. One of them is my crèche: K'liir.

            Metadata

                  63.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Githyanki are hatched in crèches all throughout Realmspace. K'liir is one of many.

                  Metadata

                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 229c7f96-a0fa-4b8e-ba63-7e27b3d7ee84Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h3ce4d52egf9d9g41b8g9626g583343e00edfText UUID: 7561bf93-811d-4ec0-8083-4facaf723d81Group ID: 1b8217c2-a22a-461f-ac5b-69b16e9f99eaGroup Index: 1
                  • +
                    213.Origin_Intro_Laezel: It's there I first saw a kith'rak mount a red dragon. Where I slit my cousins' throats at the varsh's command.

                    Metadata

                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8ebb97f8-ca13-440b-bca2-e2e74228bc90Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: haf25d6d8g0760g49e3g9837gd717873707a5Text UUID: 9a7693d0-91c2-42cc-851c-e1bd569d6912Group ID: 1b8217c2-a22a-461f-ac5b-69b16e9f99eaGroup Index: 2
                    • +

      59.Origin_Intro_Laezel: As for the renegade Voss, he waits in the sewers. He could prove an obstacle if we don't take his head.

    Text

    combine=0
    • Male
    Curious. I drained you dry. Yet your body seems no more worse for wear.

    Variant 2
